The IPA III funding framework (EUR 14.2 billion for 2021-2027) includes strengthened conditionality related to democracy, human rights, and rule of law, with provisions for suspension of assistance in cases of government backtracking. While this single incident may not trigger suspension, the pattern of enforcement failure combined with accountability avoidance creates cumulative risk. Montenegro recently received EUR 10.2 million under the Reform and Growth Facility, with remaining tranches conditional on reform commitments. Continued failure on rule of law enforcement could jeopardize future disbursements.

The scandal also undermines Montenegro's standing relative to other candidate countries. As the most advanced candidate in the Western Balkans negotiation process, Montenegro's credibility depends on demonstrating that it has moved beyond the institutional capture and enforcement failures that characterize less advanced candidates. This episode suggests the opposite.

The European Court of Auditors' 2022 special report on EU support for the rule of law in the Western Balkans already found that EU support had limited impact in improving rule of law fundamentals. The Medenica scandal provides fresh ammunition for skeptics within the EU who question whether Montenegro's reforms are substantive rather than performative.

From a regional cooperation perspective, the Interpol Red Notice for Medenica highlights the transnational dimension. If Medenica has fled to a neighboring country, it raises questions about cross-border law enforcement cooperation -- another area assessed under Chapter 24. The spectacle of a fugitive allegedly mocking police from social media while an Interpol notice remains active is reputationally devastating.
key_concerns: Ch.23 Judiciary and Fundamental Rights -- The escape of a convicted organized crime figure under active police supervision demonstrates fundamental enforcement failure, directly contradicting closing benchmarks requiring a credible track record on prosecution and sentencing enforcement for high-level organized crime cases.

Ch.24 Justice, Freedom and Security -- Police failure to enforce house arrest, the Police Director's refusal to accept responsibility, and the inability to locate the fugitive despite an Interpol Red Notice expose critical weaknesses in internal security and law enforcement capacity that the EC evaluates under this chapter.

Ch.23/24 Closing Timeline -- The 2025 EC Progress Report warned that without 'substantial acceleration of reforms' closing all chapters by 2026 would remain out of reach. This scandal moves Montenegro further from that goal at the worst possible moment.

Government Accountability Vacuum -- The absence of dismissals, the PM's departure to the UAE, and official silence on concrete measures signal institutional tolerance of impunity, which the European Commission will note in its 2026 progress assessment.

Pattern of Enforcement Failure -- The near-simultaneous escape of former special prosecutor Lidija Mitrovic suggests systemic rather than isolated failure in sentence enforcement, compounding the negative signal to Brussels.

IPA III and Reform and Growth Facility Conditionality -- Continued rule of law backsliding risks triggering enhanced scrutiny or conditionality mechanisms under IPA III, which explicitly allows suspension of assistance for democratic and rule of law regression.

Reputational Damage to Frontrunner Status -- As the most advanced Western Balkans candidate, Montenegro's credibility premium depends on demonstrating institutional maturity. This scandal erodes that premium and may slow political will within EU member states to advance Montenegro's dossier.
recommendations: Immediately dismiss the Police Director and other senior officials responsible for enforcement of the house arrest measure. Accountability is a prerequisite for credibility with the European Commission on Chapter 23 and 24 benchmarks.

Launch an independent criminal investigation into the circumstances of Medenica's escape, as demanded by Human Rights Action, including potential criminal negligence under Article 395 of the Criminal Code and possible facilitation of escape.

Present a comprehensive remedial action plan to the EU Delegation in Podgorica and the European Commission within 30 days, detailing concrete reforms to sentence enforcement procedures, electronic monitoring for high-risk detainees, and internal police accountability mechanisms.
Establish mandatory electronic monitoring (GPS ankle bracelet) for all individuals under house arrest who face sentences exceeding 5 years, closing the enforcement gap that enabled this escape.

Convene an emergency session of the National Council for European Integration to assess the damage to the accession timeline and agree on cross-party corrective measures, demonstrating to Brussels that rule of law is not a partisan issue.

Request a technical assistance mission from the EU (potentially funded under IPA III) to audit Montenegro's sentence enforcement system and recommend structural improvements aligned with EU member state best practices.

The Prime Minister should personally brief EU Ambassador Sattler and issue a detailed public statement on accountability measures, rather than delegating or remaining silent, to demonstrate political ownership of rule of law reforms.

counter_proposal.title: Emergency Rule of Law Recovery Plan
counter_proposal.summary: Rather than government silence and avoidance, the Ministry of EU Integration would implement an immediate, transparent accountability and reform response designed to convert this crisis into a demonstration of institutional self-correction -- a quality the European Commission values highly in candidate countries. The plan prioritizes visible accountability, systemic reform of sentence enforcement, and proactive engagement with EU institutions to minimize damage to the accession timeline.
key_changes: Immediate dismissal of the Police Director and heads of relevant police units, with public explanation of the basis for accountability.
Establishment of a special commission with civil society participation (including Human Rights Action) to investigate all aspects of the escape and report within 60 days.
Adoption of mandatory electronic monitoring for all high-risk detainees under house arrest, with real-time GPS tracking and automated alerts to both police and courts.
Creation of a dedicated sentence enforcement unit within the Police Administration, separate from general policing, with specialized training and clear legal authority.
Proactive briefing to the European Commission and EU Delegation presenting these measures as evidence of Montenegro's capacity for institutional self-correction.
Fast-track amendment of the Criminal Procedure Code to clarify police enforcement obligations for house arrest, eliminating the legal ambiguity the Police Director exploited.
Public commitment by the Prime Minister to a monthly progress briefing on Chapter 23 and 24 reform implementation until the chapters are closed.
expected_benefits: Demonstrates institutional self-correction capacity, which the EC weighs positively in progress assessments.
Restores credibility on Chapter 23 and 24 benchmarks by showing enforcement failures trigger consequences.
Prevents the scandal from becoming a permanent reference point in future EC progress reports.
Preserves the possibility of closing additional chapters in 2026 by showing proactive crisis management.
Strengthens the argument for continued IPA III disbursements by demonstrating reform commitment.
Builds public trust in rule of law institutions, which the EC monitors through civil society consultations.
Creates a replicable model for institutional accountability that can be applied to future failures.
counter_proposal.estimated_feasibility: High feasibility. All proposed measures are within the government's existing legal and institutional authority. Dismissals require only political will. Electronic monitoring technology is commercially available and already used in EU member states. Criminal Procedure Code amendments can be fast-tracked through parliamentary procedure. The main barrier is not capacity but political willingness to accept accountability -- which is precisely what the EU is testing.
counter_proposal.summary_mne: Umjesto šutnje i izbjegavanja od strane vlade, Ministarstvo evropskih integracija bi sprovelo neposrednu, transparentnu odgovornost i reformski odgovor, osmišljen da ovu krizu pretvori u demonstraciju institucionalne sposobnosti samokorekcije — kvalitet koji Evropska komisija visoko vrednuje kod zemalja kandidata. Plan daje prioritet vidljivoj odgovornosti, sistemskoj reformi izvršenja kazni i proaktivnom angažmanu s evropskim institucijama, kako bi se minimizovala šteta po dinamiku pristupanja.
key_changes_mne: Hitna smjena direktora Policije i rukovodilaca relevantnih policijskih jedinica, uz javno obrazloženje osnova za utvrđivanje odgovornosti.
Uspostavljanje posebne komisije uz učešće civilnog društva (uključujući Akciju za ljudska prava) radi istrage svih aspekata bjekstva, s obavezom izvještavanja u roku od 60 dana.

Uvođenje obaveznog elektronskog nadzora za sve pritvorenice i pritvorenike visokog rizika kojima je određen kućni pritvor, s praćenjem putem GPS-a u realnom vremenu i automatskim upozorenjima policiji i sudovima.
Osnivanje posebne jedinice za izvršenje kazni unutar Uprave policije, odvojene od opštih policijskih poslova, sa specijalizovanom obukom i jasnim zakonskim ovlašćenjima.
Proaktivno informisanje Evropske komisije i Delegacije EU o ovim mjerama, kao dokazu sposobnosti Crne Gore za institucionalnu samokorekciju.
Ubrzana izmjena Zakonika o krivičnom postupku radi razjašnjenja policijskih obaveza u izvršenju kućnog pritvora, čime bi se otklonila pravna nejasnoća koju je direktor Policije iskoristio.
Javna obaveza predsjednika Vlade da će mjesečno izvještavati o napretku u sprovođenju reformi iz Poglavlja 23 i 24, sve do zatvaranja tih poglavlja.
expected_benefits_mne: Dokazuje sposobnost institucionalne samokorekcije, što EK pozitivno vrednuje u ocjenama napretka.
Obnavlja kredibilitet u pogledu mjerila iz Poglavlja 23 i 24, pokazujući da propusti u izvršenju povlače posljedice.
Sprečava da skandal postane trajna referentna tačka u budućim izvještajima EK o napretku.
Čuva mogućnost zatvaranja dodatnih poglavlja u 2026. godini, demonstrirajući proaktivno upravljanje krizom.
Jača argument za nastavak isplate sredstava u okviru IPA III, pokazujući opredijeljenost za reforme.
Gradi povjerenje javnosti u institucije vladavine prava, koje EK prati kroz konsultacije s civilnim društvom.
Stvara model institucionalne odgovornosti koji se može ponoviti i primijeniti na buduće propuste.
counter_proposal.estimated_feasibility_mne: Visoka izvodljivost. Sve predložene mjere nalaze se u okviru postojećih zakonskih i institucionalnih nadležnosti vlade. Za smjene je potrebna isključivo politička volja. Tehnologija elektronskog nadzora komercijalno je dostupna i već se koristi u državama članicama EU. Izmjene Zakonika o krivičnom postupku mogu se ubrzano provesti kroz parlamentarnu proceduru. Glavna prepreka nije nedostatak kapaciteta, već politička spremnost da se prihvati odgovornost — a upravo to EU stavlja na ispit.

debate.consensus_summary: All eight ministries assessed the Medenica fugitive scandal as negative or strongly negative, with scores ranging from 2 to 3 out of 10. There is unanimous agreement on several core points: (1) the escape of a convicted organized crime figure from house arrest represents a fundamental failure of state enforcement capacity; (2) the government's absence of concrete accountability measures -- no dismissals, no criminal investigation, no transparent remediation plan -- compounds the operational failure with a political accountability crisis; (3) Montenegro's EU accession timeline, particularly Chapters 23 and 24, is directly and severely jeopardized at the worst possible moment; (4) the Police Director's refusal to resign and the PM's departure to the UAE during the crisis signal institutional dysfunction that damages Montenegro's international credibility; (5) the summoning of columnist Dusko Kovacevic to police for criticizing the Police Director constitutes an unacceptable attack on press freedom; and (6) the near-simultaneous escape of former special prosecutor Lidija Mitrovic indicates systemic rather than isolated enforcement failure. Every ministry, regardless of sector, identified cascading consequences for their domain: from investor confidence and credit ratings (Finance, Economy), to brain drain (Education, Health), to environmental enforcement credibility (Environment), to tourism reputation (Tourism). The Interior and Justice ministries delivered the harshest assessments at 2 out of 10, identifying the scandal as the most severe institutional failure within their core mandates in recent Montenegrin history.
disagreements: While the Ministry of Interior focuses on operational failures within the police chain of command and advocates immediate dismissal of the Police Director, the Ministry of Justice frames the crisis primarily as a collapse of judicial enforcement authority and emphasizes the need for an independent commission of inquiry including civil society -- the two ministries differ on whether the primary institutional failure lies with police operations or with the broader enforcement framework.

The Ministry of EU Integration treats the scandal almost exclusively through the lens of Chapter 23 and 24 benchmarks and accession timeline risk, while other ministries such as Finance and Economy emphasize market and investor confidence impacts that exist independently of the EU accession process -- there is tension between treating this as primarily an EU credibility crisis versus a broader governance and economic confidence crisis.

The Ministry of Environment advocates using the scandal as a catalyst for integrated enforcement reform that links security sector failures to environmental enforcement gaps, while other ministries treat the environmental enforcement dimension as secondary -- there is disagreement on whether the institutional reform response should be narrowly focused on the security sector or broadened to address enforcement failures across all regulatory domains.

Several ministries (Finance, Economy, Tourism) recommend proactive engagement with international partners, rating agencies, and investors to manage reputational damage, while the Interior and Justice ministries prioritize domestic accountability actions first -- there is a sequencing disagreement about whether external communications or internal accountability should take precedence.

The Ministry of Education uniquely recommends integrating the scandal into civic education curricula as a case study, which other ministries do not address -- there is an implicit disagreement about whether the government should publicly frame this crisis as a pedagogical moment or avoid amplifying institutional failure narratives.

The Ministry of Health raises concerns about social protection service continuity during political turbulence that no other ministry addresses, reflecting a disagreement about whether crisis management should explicitly include contingency planning for vulnerable populations.
debate.debate_transcript:
PARLIAMENTARY DEBATE TRANSCRIPT -- Skupstina Crne Gore
Subject: Medenica Fugitive Scandal and Government Accountability Crisis
Date of Debate: Following the decision period of 16 February 2026

MODERATOR OPENING:

The session is called to address the government's handling of the escape of convicted organized crime figure Milos Medenica from house arrest, the absence of accountability measures, and the implications for Montenegro's institutional integrity and EU accession prospects. Eight ministry assessments have been received, all rating the government's handling as negative or strongly negative. The floor is open.

---

I. OPENING STATEMENTS BY PARTY BLOCS

The ruling Europe Now! coalition opens by acknowledging the gravity of the situation. The coalition states that the conviction of Milos Medenica -- the son of former Supreme Court president Vesna Medenica, who was sanctioned by the US State Department in September 2025 for corruption -- was itself a landmark achievement in the fight against organized crime. The coalition argues that this prosecution, which began with arrests in April 2022 following the Sky ECC encrypted communications revelations, would never have occurred under previous governments. The coalition maintains that the enforcement failure lies with specific operational failures within the Police Administration, not with the government's political commitment to the rule of law. However, the coalition's position is immediately undermined by the fact that the Prime Minister departed for the UAE in the midst of the crisis, and that no concrete accountability measures have been announced as of the date of this debate.

The DPS-led opposition responds with a forceful condemnation. The opposition argues that this is not merely a police operational failure but a total collapse of state authority. The opposition cites President Milatovic's demand that the Prime Minister either dismiss those responsible or accept political responsibility himself. The opposition notes that a convicted organized crime figure has been publicly mocking the police on social media and appeared on TV Vijesti's Nacisto programme, while the government has offered nothing beyond silence. The opposition invokes the assessment of former Prime Minister Abazovic, who called this the "total collapse of the security system" and the "most bungled government in history." The opposition demands an emergency parliamentary inquiry, the immediate dismissal of the Police Director, and a vote of no confidence if the government cannot demonstrate accountability within 14 days.

The DF/DNP bloc takes the floor to argue that this scandal exposes the fundamental weakness of institutions that the ruling coalition and the DPS before it built on patronage rather than competence. The bloc contends that the focus on EU accession benchmarks misses the deeper problem: Montenegro's security institutions have been hollowed out by decades of political appointments and criminal infiltration. The bloc argues that the escape could not have occurred without internal facilitation, and demands an investigation into whether elements within the police deliberately enabled Medenica's flight. The bloc notes that while the government chases EU chapter benchmarks, it cannot perform the basic function of keeping convicted criminals in custody. The bloc calls for a comprehensive overhaul of security institutions rather than cosmetic personnel changes designed to appease Brussels.

The Bosnjacka stranka and minority party representatives express alarm that the rule-of-law collapse disproportionately affects minority communities who depend on institutional integrity for protection of their rights. The minority bloc notes that if the state cannot enforce a sentence against the son of a former Supreme Court president connected to organized crime, ordinary citizens and minority communities have even less reason to trust that institutions will protect them. The bloc supports the calls for accountability and emphasizes that EU accession -- with its requirements for minority rights protection, anti-discrimination enforcement, and institutional reform -- must not be delayed by government incompetence.

---

II. EXAMINATION OF MINISTRY ASSESSMENTS

The Ministry of Interior assessment is presented first, carrying the most severe rating of 2 out of 10. The assessment identifies the escape as the most severe institutional failure within the Ministry's core mandate in recent history, noting that the Police Administration breached its statutory obligation to enforce house arrest supervision, border control systems failed to prevent a convicted criminal from likely leaving the country, and the Police Director's refusal to resign creates an accountability vacuum. The Ministry further flags the summoning of columnist Dusko Kovacevic to police as a deeply authoritarian instinct -- turning enforcement power against critics rather than the fugitive. The Ministry raises the possibility that the escape was deliberately facilitated by individuals within the police.

The DPS-led opposition seizes on this assessment, arguing that when a government's own Interior Ministry rates its performance at 2 out of 10, the case for ministerial dismissals is self-evident. The opposition notes that Human Rights Action has formally demanded a criminal investigation under Article 395 of the Criminal Code, and that the High Court itself has confirmed that police were legally obligated to prevent the escape. The opposition asks: if the High Court says the police failed their legal duty, and the police director says he has no obligation, who governs this country -- the courts or the police director?

The ruling coalition responds by noting that the Police Director serves in a professional, not political, capacity, and that dismissal procedures must follow legal process. The coalition acknowledges that the summoning of Kovacevic was inappropriate and states that press freedom is a non-negotiable value. However, the opposition counters that legal process has not even begun -- no disciplinary proceedings, no criminal investigation, no suspension pending inquiry.

URA, as a junior coalition partner, breaks slightly from the Europe Now! position. URA emphasizes its green-liberal commitment to institutional transparency and states that the Police Director should be suspended pending investigation, that the Kovacevic summons must be formally withdrawn with an apology, and that a parliamentary commission of inquiry should be established immediately. URA argues that the coalition's credibility depends on visible accountability, not procedural delay.

The Ministry of Justice assessment, also at 2 out of 10, is presented next. The assessment characterizes the scandal as a catastrophic failure of the rule of law and notes that the case involves the son of a former Supreme Court president convicted of protecting his criminal network -- meaning failure to enforce the sentence effectively validates the corrupt nexus between organized crime and the judiciary that the prosecution was meant to dismantle.

The DF/DNP bloc responds to this assessment by arguing that the justice system has been captured by competing political factions for decades. The bloc contends that while Vesna Medenica's conviction was presented as a triumph of the rule of law, the subsequent escape of her son reveals that the system punishes individuals selectively without building genuine institutional capacity. The bloc demands to know why Medenica was placed under house arrest rather than in custody when he was convicted of creating a criminal organization -- a question that implicates the courts as much as the police.

The Ministry of EU Integration assessment, the third strongly negative rating at 2 out of 10, frames the scandal as one of the most damaging episodes for Montenegro's accession process in recent years. The assessment warns that the 2025 EC Progress Report already cautioned that without substantial acceleration of reforms, closing all chapters by 2026 would remain out of reach, and that this scandal moves Montenegro further from that goal.

The DPS-led opposition argues that Montenegro's frontrunner status among Western Balkans candidates is now in serious jeopardy. The opposition notes that the near-simultaneous escape of former special prosecutor Lidija Mitrovic -- who failed to report for a seven-month sentence for abuse of office -- indicates a systemic pattern. Two high-profile convicts walking away from sentences in the same week, the opposition argues, will feature prominently in the European Commission's 2026 progress assessment and may delay chapter closure indefinitely.

The ruling coalition responds that Montenegro's EU accession trajectory should not be held hostage to individual enforcement failures, and that the institutional reforms undertaken since 2023 -- including judicial appointments, prosecutorial independence, and anti-corruption framework legislation -- represent genuine progress that should be evaluated in context. The coalition proposes presenting a comprehensive remedial action plan to the EU Delegation within 30 days.

The Ministry of Finance assessment, rated 3 out of 10, warns of cascading economic consequences: potential delay of the S&P credit rating upgrade from B+, risk to the EUR 890 million FDI achieved in 2024, and jeopardy to hundreds of millions in EU structural and pre-accession funds. The assessment notes that Montenegro's euroization without ECB membership means the country cannot absorb fiscal shocks through monetary policy, making governance failures that raise borrowing costs particularly damaging.

The Ministry of Economy assessment, also at 3 out of 10, echoes these concerns and adds that the scandal will accelerate brain drain of skilled workers and damage investor due diligence perceptions. The Ministry of Tourism assessment warns that the scandal threatens a once-in-a-generation opportunity, with BBC Travel having named Montenegro a top 2026 destination -- reputational damage at the start of the booking season could squander this visibility.

The ruling coalition acknowledges these economic concerns and argues that swift accountability action, rather than prolonged political crisis, is the best way to protect economic interests. The coalition notes that tourism arrivals and FDI are driven by fundamentals that remain strong, and that institutional self-correction would actually demonstrate democratic maturity to international observers.

The DPS-led opposition rejects this framing, arguing that "swift accountability action" has been promised for weeks with nothing delivered. The opposition notes that the Prime Minister's departure to the UAE -- a country with which the government has separately signed a controversial development deal that former PM Abazovic has challenged as suspending Montenegrin laws -- raises additional questions about priorities and judgment.

The Ministry of Education assessment raises the alarm about brain drain acceleration, noting that 50 percent of Montenegrin youth already express desire to emigrate and that visible institutional collapse removes remaining reasons to stay. The Ministry of Health assessment warns of cascading effects on healthcare workforce retention and public trust in a system already perceived as corrupt by 60 percent of citizens. The Ministry of Environment assessment argues that the enforcement failure mirrors and reinforces the chronic enforcement gap in environmental regulation, noting that a government that cannot enforce a house arrest order cannot credibly claim it will enforce Environmental Impact Assessments or combat illegal coastal construction.

URA takes particular interest in the Environment Ministry's assessment, arguing that the enforcement capacity deficit must be addressed holistically. URA contends that the same institutional weaknesses that allowed Medenica to escape also allow illegal coastal construction in the Boka Kotorska area, waste dumping near protected areas, and pollution from the Pljevlja thermal power plant to go unchecked. URA advocates for an integrated enforcement reform that addresses security, judicial, and regulatory enforcement together.

---

III. CIVIL SOCIETY AND PUBLIC REACTION

Multiple parties reference civil society positions in support of their arguments. Human Rights Action's formal demand for a criminal investigation under Article 395 of the Criminal Code is cited repeatedly. The opposition notes that HRA characterized the Police Director's public claim that police had no further obligations as legally untenable and potentially constituting criminal negligence. HRA's condemnation of the Kovacevic police summons as intimidation -- contrary to European Court of Human Rights standards -- is cited by URA and the opposition as evidence that the security apparatus is turning against democratic oversight rather than pursuing the fugitive.

International media coverage by OCCRP, Balkan Insight, and others is referenced by the Finance and Economy-focused arguments, with the opposition noting that the Balkan Insight headline -- "High-Profile Convict Escapes Embarrass Montenegro at Crucial EU Juncture" -- will be read by every EU official, investor, and rating agency analyst following Montenegro.

The DF/DNP bloc cites the media coverage to argue that Montenegro's international image has been damaged not by the escape alone but by the pattern of governance failure it reveals. The bloc argues that both the ruling coalition and the previous DPS government bear responsibility for building institutions that prioritize political loyalty over professional competence.

---

IV. KEY POINTS OF CONTENTION

First, the question of accountability scope. The ruling coalition frames this as a police operational failure requiring targeted personnel changes. The DPS-led opposition frames it as a systemic governance failure requiring ministerial accountability and potentially a no-confidence vote. The DF/DNP bloc frames it as evidence of deep institutional capture requiring comprehensive security sector reform. These three framings lead to fundamentally different prescriptions.

Second, the question of EU accession implications. The ruling coalition argues that Montenegro can self-correct and that the EU will evaluate the response alongside the broader reform record. The opposition argues that the damage is already done and that Montenegro's Chapter 23/24 closure timeline for Q3 2026 is now effectively impossible. The DF/DNP bloc questions whether EU benchmarks should be the primary lens through which institutional reform is evaluated, arguing that security institutions should serve citizens, not Brussels checklists.

Third, the question of the Prime Minister's personal responsibility. President Milatovic's public demand that the PM dismiss those responsible or resign himself has created a constitutional dynamic. The opposition supports this demand. The ruling coalition argues that the PM's authority to dismiss the Police Director must follow legal procedures. The DF/DNP bloc argues that the PM's departure to the UAE during the crisis is itself disqualifying.

Fourth, the question of press freedom. All parties formally condemn the Kovacevic summons, but the opposition and civil society argue that the ruling coalition's condemnation is hollow without the Police Director's removal, since the summons originated from the security apparatus the government oversees.

---

V. VOTING ASSESSMENT AND VERDICT

Based on the unanimous negative assessments from all eight ministries, the cross-party condemnation of the government's non-response, and the gravity of the institutional failures documented, the Parliament arrives at a strongly negative overall verdict.

The ruling Europe Now!/URA coalition, while defending its broader reform record, cannot credibly defend the government's handling of this specific crisis. The absence of any concrete accountability measures -- no dismissals, no criminal investigations, no suspension of the Police Director, no public remediation plan -- in the weeks since January 28 is indefensible. The Prime Minister's departure to the UAE during the crisis compounded the impression of governmental indifference.

The DPS-led opposition, the DF/DNP bloc, and minority party representatives are unified in demanding immediate accountability, though they differ on the scope and nature of the reforms required. This rare cross-bloc consensus on the severity of the failure, combined with President Milatovic's public intervention, creates a political environment in which the government must act or face escalating institutional and political consequences.

The Parliament notes with particular concern:
(a) The High Court's explicit finding that police were obligated to prevent the escape, contradicting the Police Director's public statements;
(b) The near-simultaneous escape of former special prosecutor Lidija Mitrovic, indicating systemic enforcement failure;
(c) The intimidation of journalist Dusko Kovacevic through police summoning for legitimate criticism;
(d) The fugitive's apparent public appearances mocking state institutions while an Interpol Red Notice goes unexecuted;
(e) The cascading damage to EU accession prospects, investor confidence, and public trust documented across all ministry assessments.

The Parliament calls upon the government to immediately dismiss or suspend the Police Director, initiate criminal investigations into the enforcement failure, present a comprehensive accountability and remediation plan within 14 days, and ensure that press freedom is protected rather than threatened during this crisis. Failure to act will be interpreted as institutional tolerance of impunity and will warrant further parliamentary measures including a potential vote of no confidence.
debate.consensus_summary_mne: Svih osam ministarstava ocijenilo je skandal sa bjegom Medenice negativno ili izrazito negativno, s ocjenama od 2 do 3 od 10. Postoji jednoglasna saglasnost o nekoliko ključnih tačaka: (1) bijeg osuđenog pripadnika organizovanog kriminala s kućnog pritvora predstavlja fundamentalni propust kapaciteta državne prinude; (2) odsustvo konkretnih mjera odgovornosti od strane vlade -- bez razrješenja, bez krivične istrage, bez transparentnog plana sanacije -- operativni propust produbljuje krizom političke odgovornosti; (3) rok za pristupanje Crne Gore EU, posebno Poglavlja 23 i 24, direktno je i ozbiljno ugrožen u najgorem mogućem trenutku; (4) odbijanje direktora policije da podnese ostavku i odlazak premijera u UAE usred krize signaliziraju institucionalnu disfunkciju koja narušava međunarodni kredibilitet Crne Gore; (5) pozivanje kolumniste Duška Kovačevića u policiju zbog kritike direktora policije predstavlja neprihvatljiv napad na slobodu štampe; i (6) gotovo istovremeni bijeg bivše specijalne tužiteljke Lidije Mitrović ukazuje na sistemski, a ne izolovani propust u izvršavanju kazni. Svako ministarstvo, bez obzira na sektor, identifikovalo je kaskadne posljedice po svoju oblast: od povjerenja investitora i kreditnih rejtinga (Finansije, Ekonomija), do odljeva mozgova (Obrazovanje, Zdravlje), do vjerodostojnosti sprovođenja ekoloških propisa (Ekologija), do reputacije turizma (Turizam). Ministarstvo unutrašnjih poslova i Ministarstvo pravde iznijeli su najstrože ocjene -- 2 od 10, identifikujući skandal kao najozbiljniji institucionalni propust u okviru svojih temeljnih nadležnosti u novijoj crnogorskoj istoriji.
disagreements_mne: Dok se Ministarstvo unutrašnjih poslova fokusira na operativne propuste u policijskom lancu komandovanja i zagovara hitno razrješenje direktora policije, Ministarstvo pravde krizu sagledava primarno kao slom pravosudnog izvršnog autoriteta i naglašava potrebu za nezavisnom komisijom za istragu koja bi uključivala i civilno društvo -- ova dva ministarstva razilaze se oko toga da li se primarni institucionalni propust nalazi u radu policije ili u širem okviru izvršavanja zakona.

Ministarstvo za evropske integracije skandal razmatra gotovo isključivo kroz prizmu mjerila Poglavlja 23 i 24 i rizika po rok pristupanja, dok druga ministarstva, poput Finansija i Ekonomije, naglašavaju uticaj na tržišno i investitorsko povjerenje koji postoji nezavisno od procesa pristupanja EU -- prisutna je tenzija između tretiranja ovoga primarno kao krize kredibiliteta pred EU i šire krize upravljanja i ekonomskog povjerenja.

Ministarstvo ekologije zagovara korišćenje skandala kao katalizatora za integrisanu reformu izvršavanja zakona koja bi povezala propuste u sektoru bezbjednosti s nedostacima u sprovođenju ekoloških propisa, dok ga druga ministarstva tretiraju kao sekundarno -- postoji neslaganje oko toga da li odgovor institucionalnih reformi treba da bude usko usmjeren na sektor bezbjednosti ili proširen na propuste u sprovođenju zakona u svim regulatornim oblastima.

Nekoliko ministarstava (Finansije, Ekonomija, Turizam) preporučuje proaktivno angažovanje s međunarodnim partnerima, agencijama za kreditni rejting i investitorima radi upravljanja reputacionom štetom, dok Ministarstvo unutrašnjih poslova i Ministarstvo pravde prioritizuju domaće mjere odgovornosti -- postoji neslaganje u pogledu redoslijeda između toga šta treba da ima prednost: eksterna komunikacija ili interna odgovornost.

Ministarstvo prosvjete jedino preporučuje uvrštavanje skandala u nastavne programe građanskog obrazovanja kao studiju slučaja, što druga ministarstva ne razmatraju -- postoji implicitno neslaganje oko toga da li vlada treba javno da prikaže ovu krizu kao pedagoški momenat ili da izbjegava pojačavanje narativa o institucionalnom propustu.

Ministarstvo zdravlja izražava zabrinutost zbog kontinuiteta usluga socijalne zaštite tokom perioda političke turbulencije, što nijedno drugo ministarstvo ne apostrofira, odražavajući neslaganje oko toga da li upravljanje krizom treba eksplicitno da uključuje planove za nepredviđene situacije kojima su izložene ugrožene grupe stanovništva.
debate.debate_transcript_mne:
TRANSKRIPT PARLAMENTARNE DEBATE -- Skupština Crne Gore
Predmet: Skandal sa bjegom Medenice i kriza vladine odgovornosti
Datum debate: Nakon odlučivanja 16. februara 2026. godine

UVODNA RIJEČ MODERATORA:

Sjednica je sazvana kako bi se razmotrilo postupanje vlade u slučaju bjega osuđenog pripadnika organizovanog kriminala Miloša Medenice s kućnog pritvora, odsustvo mjera odgovornosti i implikacije po institucionalni integritet Crne Gore i izglede za pristupanje EU. Primljene su procjene osam ministarstava, od kojih su sve ocijenile postupanje vlade negativno ili izrazito negativno. Debata je otvorena.

---

I. UVODNE IZJAVE STRANAČKIH BLOKOVA

Vladajuća koalicija Evropa sad! otvara sjednicu priznavajući ozbiljnost situacije. Koalicija navodi da je osuda Miloša Medenice -- sina bivše predsjednice Vrhovnog suda Vesne Medenice, koja je u septembru 2025. sankcionisana od strane Stejt departmenta SAD-a zbog korupcije -- sama po sebi bila istorijsko dostignuće u borbi protiv organizovanog kriminala. Koalicija tvrdi da do ovog krivičnog gonjenja, koje je počelo hapšenjima u aprilu 2022. nakon razotkrivanja šifrovane Sky ECC komunikacije, nikada ne bi došlo pod prethodnim vladama. Koalicija insistira da operativni propust leži u specifičnim operativnim greškama unutar Uprave policije, a ne u političkoj posvećenosti vlade vladavini prava. Međutim, pozicija koalicije odmah je dovedena u pitanje činjenicom da je premijer otputovao u UAE usred krize i da do datuma ove debate nisu najavljene nikakve konkretne mjere odgovornosti.

Opozicija predvođena DPS-om odgovara oštrom osudom. Opozicija tvrdi da se ne radi samo o operativnom propustu policije, već o potpunom slomu državnog autoriteta. Opozicija poziva na zahtjev predsjednika Milatovića da premijer ili razriješi odgovorne ili lično preuzme političku odgovornost. Opozicija ističe da osuđeni pripadnik organizovanog kriminala javno ismijava policiju na društvenim mrežama i pojavio se u emisiji Načisto na TV Vijesti, dok vlada nije ponudila ništa osim šutnje. Opozicija se poziva na ocjenu bivšeg premijera Abazovića, koji je ovo nazvao "potpunim slomom sistema bezbjednosti" i "najnespretnije vođenom vladom u istoriji". Opozicija zahtijeva hitnu parlamentarnu istragu, trenutno razrješenje direktora policije i glasanje o nepovjerenju ukoliko vlada ne dokaže odgovornost u roku od 14 dana.

Blok DF/DNP uzima reč kako bi iznio argument da ovaj skandal razotkriva temeljnu slabost institucija koje su vladajuća koalicija i DPS prije nje gradile na klijentelizmu, a ne na kompetentnosti. Blok tvrdi da usredisređenost na mjerila pristupanja EU previđa dublji problem: bezbjednosne institucije Crne Gore iscrpljene su decenijama političkih imenovanja i kriminalnog infiltriranja. Blok iznosi argument da bijeg nije mogao da se dogodi bez unutrašnje facilitacije i zahtijeva istragu o tome da li su određeni elementi unutar policije namjerno omogućili Medeničin bijeg. Blok napominje da dok vlada traga za EU poglavljima, nije u stanju da ispuni osnovnu funkciju čuvanja osuđenih kriminalaca u pritvoru. Blok poziva na sveobuhvatnu reformu bezbjednosnih institucija, a ne na kozmetičke kadrovske promjene osmišljene da umire Brisel.

Predstavnici Bošnjačke stranke i manjinskih stranaka izražavaju zabrinutost da slom vladavine prava nesrazmjerno pogađa manjinske zajednice koje za zaštitu svojih prava zavise od institucionalnog integriteta. Manjinski blok ističe da ako država ne može da izvrši presudu protiv sina bivše predsjednice Vrhovnog suda povezanog s organizovanim kriminalom, obični građani i manjinske zajednice imaju još manje razloga da vjeruju da će ih institucije štititi. Blok podržava pozive na odgovornost i naglašava da pristupanje EU -- s njegovim zahtjevima za zaštitu manjinskih prava, sprovođenje antidiskriminacionog zakonodavstva i institucionalnu reformu -- ne smije biti odloženo zbog vladine nekompetentnosti.

---

II. RAZMATRANJE MINISTARSKIH PROCJENA

Prvo se iznosi procjena Ministarstva unutrašnjih poslova, s najtežom ocjenom -- 2 od 10. Procjena identifikuje bijeg kao najozbiljniji institucionalni propust u okviru temeljnog mandata Ministarstva u novijoj istoriji, napominjući da je Uprava policije prekršila svoju zakonsku obavezu nadzora kućnog pritvora, da sistemi granične kontrole nisu uspjeli spriječiti vjerovatni odlazak osuđenog kriminalca iz zemlje i da direktoru policije koji odbija da podnese ostavku stvara vakuum odgovornosti. Ministarstvo dodatno ukazuje na pozivanje kolumniste Duška Kovačevića u policiju kao na duboko autoritarni instinkt -- usmjeravanje prinudne moći protiv kritičara umjesto protiv bjeginca. Ministarstvo postavlja pitanje da li je bijeg namjerno olakšan od strane pojedinaca unutar policije.

Opozicija predvođena DPS-om koristi ovu procjenu, tvrdeći da kada vlastito Ministarstvo unutrašnjih poslova vlade ocijeni njene učinke ocjenom 2 od 10, slučaj za ministarska razrješenja govori sam za sebe. Opozicija napominje da je Akcija za ljudska prava formalno zahtijevala krivičnu istragu po članu 395 Krivičnog zakonika i da je i Viši sud potvrdio da je policija bila zakonski obavezna spriječiti bjeg. Opozicija pita: ako Viši sud kaže da je policija prekršila svoju zakonsku dužnost, a direktor policije tvrdi da nema nikakvih obaveza, ko upravlja ovom državom -- sudovi ili direktor policije?

Vladajuća koalicija odgovara napominjući da direktor policije obnaša profesionalnu, a ne političku funkciju i da procedure razrješenja moraju slijediti zakonski postupak. Koalicija priznaje da je pozivanje Kovačevića bilo neprikladno i navodi da je sloboda štampe nepregovariva vrijednost. Međutim, opozicija uzvraća da zakonski postupak nije ni počeo -- nema disciplinskog postupka, nema krivične istrage, nema suspenzije do okončanja istrage.

URA, kao mlađi koalicioni partner, blago odstupa od pozicije Evrope sad!. URA naglašava svoju zeleno-liberalnu posvećenost institucionalnoj transparentnosti i navodi da direktor policije treba da bude suspendovan do okončanja istrage, da poziv Kovačeviću mora biti formalno povučen uz izvinjenje i da parlamentarna komisija za istragu treba biti uspostavljena odmah. URA tvrdi da kredibilitet koalicije zavisi od vidljive odgovornosti, a ne od proceduralnog odlaganja.

Zatim se iznosi procjena Ministarstva pravde, takođe s ocjenom 2 od 10. Procjena skandal karakteriše kao katastrofalni propust vladavine prava i napominje da slučaj uključuje sina bivše predsjednice Vrhovnog suda osuđenog za zaštitu svoje kriminalne mreže -- što znači da neuspjeh u izvršavanju presude efektivno validira korumpirani splet između organizovanog kriminala i pravosuđa koji je krivično gonjenje trebalo da razotkrije.

Blok DF/DNP reaguje na ovu procjenu argumentom da je pravosudni sistem decenijama bio zarobljen od strane rivalskih političkih frakcija. Blok tvrdi da je, iako je osuda Vesne Medenice predstavljena kao trijumf vladavine prava, naknadni bijeg njenog sina otkrio da sistem kažnjava pojedince selektivno, a ne da gradi pravi institucionalni kapacitet. Blok zahtijeva odgovor zašto je Medenica stavljen pod kućni pritvor umjesto u zatvor kada je osuđen za osnivanje kriminalne organizacije -- pitanje koje implicira sudove jednako kao i policiju.

Procjena Ministarstva za evropske integracije, treća izrazito negativna ocjena od 2 od 10, skandal okviruje kao jednu od najštetnijih epizoda za crnogorski pristupni proces u posljednjih nekoliko godina. Procjena upozorava da je Izvještaj EK o napretku za 2025. već bio upozorio da bi bez značajnog ubrzanja reformi zatvaranje svih poglavlja do 2026. ostalo van domašaja i da ovaj skandal Crnu Goru udaljava od tog cilja.

Opozicija predvođena DPS-om tvrdi da je status Crne Gore kao frontrunera među kandidatima Zapadnog Balkana sada u ozbiljnoj opasnosti. Opozicija napominje da gotovo istovremeni bijeg bivše specijalne tužiteljke Lidije Mitrović -- koja se nije javila na odsluženje sedmomjesečne kazne za zloupotrebu položaja -- ukazuje na sistemski obrazac. Dva visokorangirana osuđenika koji u istoj nedjelji odlaze bez posljedica, tvrdi opozicija, biće istaknuta u procjeni napretka Evropske komisije za 2026. godinu i mogu indefinitivno odložiti zatvaranje poglavlja.

Vladajuća koalicija odgovara da crnogorski put ka EU ne treba da bude taoc individualnih operativnih propusta i da institucionalne reforme preduzete od 2023. -- uključujući sudska imenovanja, nezavisnost tužilaštva i zakonodavni okvir za borbu protiv korupcije -- predstavljaju pravi napredak koji treba vrednovati u kontekstu. Koalicija predlaže predstavljanje sveobuhvatnog plana korektivnih mjera Delegaciji EU u roku od 30 dana.

Procjena Ministarstva finansija, ocijenjena s 3 od 10, upozorava na kaskadne ekonomske posljedice: potencijalno odlaganje napredovanja kreditnog rejtinga S&P s B+, rizik za 890 miliona EUR FDI ostvarenih u 2024. godini i ugroženost stotina miliona eura u EU strukturnim fondovima i fondovima pretpristupne pomoći. Procjena napominje da crnogorska eurizacija bez članstva u ESB znači da zemlja ne može apsorbovati fiskalne šokove kroz monetarnu politiku, što propuste u upravljanju koji povisuju troškove zaduživanja čini posebno štetnim.

Procjena Ministarstva ekonomije, takođe ocijenjenog s 3 od 10, odražava ove brige i dodaje da će skandal ubrzati odljev stručnih radnika i narušiti percepcije investitora tokom due diligence procesa. Procjena Ministarstva turizma upozorava da skandal prijeti šansom koja se ne pruža često, budući da je BBC Travel proglasio Crnu Goru jednom od top destinacija za 2026. godinu -- reputaciona šteta na početku sezone rezervacija mogla bi rasipati ovu vidljivost.

Vladajuća koalicija uznaje ove ekonomske brige i tvrdi da brza akcija odgovornosti, a ne produžena politička kriza, predstavlja najbolji način zaštite ekonomskih interesa. Koalicija napominje da turistički dolasci i FDI zavise od fundamentala koji ostaju snažni i da bi institucionalna samokorekcija zapravo demonstrirala demokratsku zrelost međunarodnim posmatračima.

Opozicija predvođena DPS-om odbacuje ovaj okvir, tvrdeći da se "brza akcija odgovornosti" obećava sedmicama, a da ništa nije učinjeno. Opozicija napominje da odlazak premijera u UAE -- državu s kojom je vlada posebno potpisala kontroverzni razvojni sporazum koji bivši premijer Abazović osporio kao suspenziju crnogorskih zakona -- postavlja dodatna pitanja o prioritetima i prosuđivanju.

Procjena Ministarstva prosvjete uzdiže uzbunu zbog ubrzanja odljeva mozgova, napominjući da 50 posto crnogorske omladine već izražava želju za emigriranjem i da vidljivi institucionalni slom uklanja preostale razloge za ostanak. Procjena Ministarstva zdravlja upozorava na kaskadne efekte na zadržavanje zdravstvenih radnika i javno povjerenje u sistem koji 60 posto građana već percipira kao korumpiran. Procjena Ministarstva ekologije tvrdi da operativni propust zrcali i pojačava hronični jaz u sprovođenju ekoloških propisa, napominjući da vlada koja ne može da sprovede nalog o kućnom pritvoru ne može vjerodostojno tvrditi da će sprovoditi Procjene uticaja na životnu sredinu ili se boriti protiv ilegalne gradnje na primorju.

URA posebno pridaje pažnju procjeni Ministarstva ekologije, tvrdeći da deficit kapaciteta izvršavanja zakona mora biti rješavan sveobuhvatno. URA tvrdi da iste institucionalne slabosti koje su Medenici omogućile bijeg istovremeno dozvoljavaju ilegalnu gradnju na primorju u oblasti Boke Kotorske, odlaganje otpada u blizini zaštićenih područja i onečišćenje iz termoelektrane u Pljevljima da prođu nekažnjeno. URA zagovara integrisanu reformu izvršavanja zakona koja bi zajedno tretirala bezbjednosno, pravosudno i regulatorno izvršavanje.

---

III. REAKCIJE CIVILNOG DRUŠTVA I JAVNOSTI

Višestruke stranke pozivaju se na stavove civilnog društva u prilog svojih argumenata. Formalni zahtjev Akcije za ljudska prava za pokretanje krivične istrage po članu 395 Krivičnog zakonika citira se više puta. Opozicija napominje da je ALP karakterisala javnu izjavu direktora policije da policija nema daljih obaveza kao pravno neodržive i potencijalno krivično nemar. Osuda ALP-a pozivanja Kovačevića policiji kao zastrašivanja -- suprotnog standardima Evropskog suda za ljudska prava -- citira se od strane URA-e i opozicije kao dokaz da se bezbjednosni aparat okreće protiv demokratskog nadzora umjesto da traga za bjeguncem.

Na medijsku pokrivenost OCCRP-a, Balkan Insighta i drugih pozivaju se argumenti fokusirani na finansije i ekonomiju, a opozicija napominje da će naslov Balkan Insighta -- "Bjegovi visokorangiranih osuđenika kompromituju Crnu Goru u ključnom trenutku za EU" -- pročitati svaki zvaničnik EU, investitor i analitičar rejting agencija koji prati Crnu Goru.

Blok DF/DNP citira medijsku pokrivenost kako bi iznio argument da je međunarodni imidž Crne Gore oštećen ne samo samim bjegom, već obrascem propusta u upravljanju koji on otkriva. Blok tvrdi da i vladajuća koalicija i prethodna DPS-ova vlada snose odgovornost za izgradnju institucija koje prioritizuju političku lojalnost nad stručnom kompetencijom.

---

IV. KLJUČNE TAČKE SPORA

Prvo, pitanje opsega odgovornosti. Vladajuća koalicija ovo okviruje kao operativni policijski propust koji zahtijeva ciljane kadrovske promjene. Opozicija predvođena DPS-om to okviruje kao sistemski propust upravljanja koji zahtijeva ministarsku odgovornost i potencijalno glasanje o nepovjerenju. Blok DF/DNP to okviruje kao dokaz duboke institucionalne zarobljenosti koja zahtijeva sveobuhvatnu reformu sektora bezbjednosti. Ova tri okvira vode ka fundamentalno različitim preskripcijama.

Drugo, pitanje implikacija na pristupanje EU. Vladajuća koalicija tvrdi da Crna Gora može izvršiti samokorekciju i da će EU odgovor vrednovati uz širu reformsku evidenciju. Opozicija tvrdi da je šteta već učinjena i da je rok za zatvaranje Poglavlja 23/24 Crne Gore u trećem kvartalu 2026. sada efektivno nemoguć. Blok DF/DNP dovodi u pitanje da li mjerila EU treba da budu primarni objektiv kroz koji se vrednuje institucionalna reforma, tvrdeći da bezbjednosne institucije treba da služe građanima, a ne briselskim kontrolnim listama.

Treće, pitanje lične odgovornosti premijera. Javni zahtjev predsjednika Milatovića da premijer razriješi odgovorne ili da i sam podnese ostavku stvorio je ustavnu dinamiku. Opozicija podržava ovaj zahtjev. Vladajuća koalicija tvrdi da se premijeru ovlašćenje za razrješenje direktora policije mora pridržavati zakonskih procedura. Blok DF/DNP tvrdi da je odlazak premijera u UAE usred krize sam po sebi diskvalifikujući.

Četvrto, pitanje slobode štampe. Sve stranke formalno osuđuju pozivanje Kovačevića, ali opozicija i civilno društvo tvrde da je osuda vladajuće koalicije prazna bez smjene direktora policije, budući da poziv potiče od bezbjednosnog aparata kojim vlada rukovodi.

---

V. OCJENA GLASANJA I PRESUDA

Na osnovu jednoglasno negativnih procjena svih osam ministarstava, međustranačke osude vladine ne-reakcije i ozbiljnosti dokumentovanih institucionalnih propusta, Skupština donosi izrazito negativnu opštu presudu.

Vladajuća koalicija Evropa sad!/URA, dok brani svoju širu reformsku evidenciju, ne može uvjerljivo braniti vladino postupanje u ovoj konkretnoj krizi. Odsustvo ikakvih konkretnih mjera odgovornosti -- bez razrješenja, bez krivičnih istraga, bez suspenzije direktora policije, bez javnog plana sanacije -- u sedmicama od 28. januara neodbranjivo je. Odlazak premijera u UAE usred krize pogoršao je utisak o vladnoj indiferentnosti.

Opozicija predvođena DPS-om, blok DF/DNP i predstavnici manjinskih stranaka ujedinjeni su u zahtjevu za trenutnom odgovornošću, iako se razilaze po pitanju opsega i prirode neophodnih reformi. Ova rijetka međublokovska saglasnost o ozbiljnosti propusta, u kombinaciji s javnom intervencijom predsjednika Milatovića, stvara političko okruženje u kojem vlada mora da djeluje ili da se suoči s eskalacijom institucionalnih i političkih posljedica.

Skupština s posebnom zabrinutošću konstatuje:
(a) Eksplicitni nalaz Višeg suda da je policija bila obavezna spriječiti bijeg, koji protivurječi javnim izjavama direktora policije;
(b) Gotovo istovremeni bijeg bivše specijalne tužiteljke Lidije Mitrović, koji ukazuje na sistemski propust u izvršavanju;
(c) Zastrašivanje novinara Duška Kovačevića putem policijskog pozivanja zbog legitimne kritike;
(d) Navodne javne pojave bjeginca koji se ruga državnim institucijama dok Interpolova crvena potjernica ostaje neizvršena;
(e) Kaskadnu štetu po izglede za pristupanje EU, povjerenje investitora i javno povjerenje dokumentovanu u svim ministarskim procjenama.

Skupština poziva vladu da odmah razriješi ili suspenduje direktora policije, pokrene krivične istrage u vezi s operativnim propustom, predstavi sveobuhvatan plan odgovornosti i sanacije u roku od 14 dana i osigura da sloboda štampe bude zaštićena, a ne ugrožena tokom ove krize. Nečinjenje će biti protumačeno kao institucionalna tolerancija nekažnjivosti i zahtijevaće dalje parlamentarne mjere, uključujući potencijalno glasanje o nepovjerenju.

blind_spots: No ministry questioned why Medenica was on house arrest rather than in custody in the first place. He was released from Spuz detention in November 2025 despite facing a 10-year sentence for leading a criminal organization. The judiciary's decision to grant house arrest to a high-flight-risk organized crime defendant -- whose mother was the former Supreme Court president with documented connections to his criminal network -- represents a glaring upstream failure that every ministry ignored.

The possibility of deliberate facilitation of the escape by insiders was mentioned only in passing by the Ministry of Interior. Given that Medenica's criminal network included a police officer (Darko Lalovic) who helped arrange cocaine purchases per Sky ECC transcripts, and given that his mother ran the Supreme Court for nearly two decades building patronage networks, the question of whether police or judicial insiders actively assisted the escape deserved central rather than peripheral analysis.

No ministry examined the coalition politics dimension. PM Spajic leads a fragile coalition government that has already experienced crises over police director appointments (Balkan Insight reported a government split over the police chief appointment in March 2024). The inability to dismiss the Police Director may reflect coalition constraints rather than mere indifference -- a distinction with practical implications for reform prospects.

No ministry proposed concrete institutional reforms to prevent recurrence. Electronic monitoring ankle bracelets, GPS tracking, mandatory pre-sentencing custody for flight-risk defendants, and reform of house arrest enforcement protocols are standard measures in EU member states. The absence of any forward-looking policy recommendations makes the assessments diagnostic but not prescriptive.

The PM's specific destination -- the UAE -- was noted but never interrogated. Montenegro has complex financial relationships with Gulf states, and the timing of a UAE visit during a domestic crisis of this magnitude raises questions about competing priorities and foreign policy dependencies that no ministry addressed.

No ministry analyzed the chilling effect on pending organized crime cases. If other high-profile defendants see that a convicted crime boss can vanish with impunity, the rational calculus for fleeing rather than serving sentences shifts dramatically. This precedent-setting dimension -- potentially affecting dozens of active cases -- was entirely overlooked.

The regional dimension was underexplored. Medenica likely crossed an international border, but no ministry analyzed which country likely received him, what this reveals about cross-border law enforcement gaps, or whether neighboring states may be complicit. The Interpol Red Notice implies international flight, yet the regional cooperation failures remain unexamined.

All seven ministries scored the decision between 2 and 3 out of 10, with identical 'negative' or 'strongly_negative' verdicts and remarkably similar analytical frameworks centered on EU accession. This uniformity across ministries with very different mandates (Education, Health, Environment, Tourism) suggests coordinated messaging rather than genuinely independent domain-specific analysis. A truly independent Education ministry might focus more on civic education impacts; a Health ministry on specific health system corruption cases.
critic_report.overall_analysis:
INDEPENDENT ASSESSMENT: This is not merely a security scandal -- it is a systemic governance failure that exposes the hollowness of Montenegro's reform narrative at the most critical moment in its EU accession process.

THE FACTS ARE DAMNING. Milos Medenica, head of a 16-member criminal organization involved in drug and cigarette smuggling, was last confirmed at his house arrest address at 5:30 PM on January 27, 2026. By the time of his sentencing to 10 years and 2 months the next day, he had vanished. The High Court of Podgorica confirmed that police were legally obligated to prevent his escape. Within approximately one week, former special prosecutor Lidija Mitrovic also failed to report for her seven-month sentence. Two high-profile fugitives in rapid succession is a pattern, not a coincidence.

THE UPSTREAM FAILURE NOBODY MENTIONS. The ministries unanimously condemn the escape but none ask the obvious prior question: why was Medenica on house arrest at all? He was released from Spuz detention in November 2025 (documented by HRA) despite leading a criminal organization, despite his mother -- former Supreme Court President Vesna Medenica, sanctioned by the US State Department in September 2025 for corruption -- having spent two decades building judicial patronage networks. The flight risk was blindingly obvious. The judiciary that allowed house arrest for this defendant shares responsibility with the police who failed to enforce it.

THE GOVERNMENT NON-RESPONSE IS WORSE THAN THE ESCAPE. President Milatovic was right to demand dismissals. The Police Director's refusal to resign after the High Court itself confirmed police bore the enforcement obligation is an act of institutional defiance. PM Spajic's departure to the UAE after a single statement is not merely bad optics -- it signals that the head of government either cannot or will not exercise authority over his own security apparatus. Former PM Abazovic's characterization of "total collapse of the security system" is substantiated by the facts.

THE PRESS FREEDOM DIMENSION COMPOUNDS THE CRISIS. The police summoning of columnist Dusko Kovacevic on February 16 for his article "Better than Escape" criticizing the Police Director is textbook authoritarian deflection. HRA documented that no written summons with reasoning was provided, Kovacevic was not informed of his right to counsel, and no specific criminal offense was cited. The Basic State Prosecutor's Office subsequently confirmed no criminal elements existed. This is the security apparatus using its enforcement power against critics while failing to enforce it against fugitives -- a chilling inversion of institutional purpose.

EU ACCESSION IMPACT. Montenegro received a positive IBAR for Chapters 23 and 24 in June 2024 and closed Chapter 32 in January 2026, building genuine momentum. That momentum is now at serious risk. EU Commissioner Kos warned that "the hardest part is still ahead" on rule-of-law chapters. The Medenica escape provides the European Commission with precisely the kind of concrete enforcement failure that justifies skepticism about Montenegro's institutional capacity. The 2025 EC Progress Report already warned that "without a substantial acceleration of reforms, closing all chapters by end of 2026 will remain out of reach." This scandal moves the needle in the wrong direction. The US sanctions against Vesna Medenica in September 2025 for enabling drug trafficking through judicial corruption, combined with her son's escape, create a compounding international credibility problem.

ASSESSMENT OF MINISTRY EVALUATIONS. The seven ministry assessments are substantively thorough and well-sourced. They correctly identify the core EU accession risk, cite relevant international sources (OCCRP, Balkan Insight, HRA, US State Department), and connect the scandal to their respective domains. However, their analytical value is diminished by three problems. First, the scoring uniformity (all between 2-3) across vastly different ministries suggests coordination rather than independent analysis. The Ministry of Justice and Ministry of Interior have direct mandate relevance and their scores of 2 are well-calibrated; but the Ministry of Tourism and Ministry of Education scoring 3 for a security scandal feels like box-ticking. Second, every ministry defaults to the same EU-accession-centric analytical framework, which while valid, produces repetitive analysis rather than genuine domain-specific insight. Third, critically, no ministry proposes concrete forward-looking reforms -- they diagnose the disease but prescribe no treatment. Not one mentions electronic monitoring, GPS ankle bracelets, mandatory pre-sentencing custody for flight risks, or any other specific institutional fix. This is analysis without actionable recommendations, which limits its governance value.

THE DEEPER QUESTION. The Medenica case reveals whether Montenegro's anti-corruption prosecutions are substantive reforms or performative exercises. Prosecuting and convicting the son of the former Supreme Court president was a landmark achievement. But a conviction that cannot be enforced is worse than no conviction at all -- it demonstrates state capacity to investigate and prosecute while simultaneously demonstrating incapacity to execute sentences. The international community and Montenegrin citizens are left with the worst possible signal: the system can identify criminals in power but cannot actually hold them accountable. This gap between prosecution and enforcement is the central governance failure that the ministries identify but do not adequately explain.

VERDICT: The government's handling of the Medenica escape -- characterized by police failure, official silence, the PM's absence, refusal of accountability, and the intimidation of a journalist -- merits a score of 2 out of 10. The only reason it does not receive a 1 is that the original prosecution and conviction of the Medenica network represented genuine institutional effort, even if that effort was subsequently negated by enforcement failure.
critic_report.headline: Montenegro Cannot Catch Its Criminals or Hold Its Officials Accountable
eu_chapter_relevance: Ch.23 Judiciary and Fundamental Rights -- The escape of a convicted organized crime figure whose mother was the former Supreme Court president, combined with the judiciary's prior decision to grant house arrest to a high-flight-risk defendant, directly undermines the closing benchmarks requiring credible track records in prosecuting and enforcing sentences for organized crime and high-level corruption. The IBAR progress from June 2024 is at risk of being reversed.

Ch.24 Justice, Freedom and Security -- Police failure to enforce house arrest, border control failure allowing international flight (evidenced by the Interpol Red Notice), the Police Director's refusal to accept responsibility, and the near-simultaneous escape of former special prosecutor Mitrovic expose systemic weaknesses in internal security, law enforcement capacity, and cross-border cooperation that are core to this chapter's closing benchmarks.

Ch.10 Information Society and Media -- The police summoning of columnist Dusko Kovacevic for criticizing the Police Director, without proper legal basis or procedural safeguards as documented by HRA, constitutes intimidation of press freedom that contradicts ECHR standards and the media freedom requirements assessed under this provisionally closed chapter. This could prompt the EU to revisit the chapter's status.

Ch.31 Foreign, Security and Defence Policy -- The active Interpol Red Notice for Medenica and the regional dimension of his escape raise questions about Montenegro's capacity for international law enforcement cooperation and its reliability as a partner in EU security frameworks.
critic_report.headline_mne: Crna Gora ne može uhvatiti svoje kriminalce niti pozvati na odgovornost svoje zvaničnike
critic_report.overall_analysis_mne:
NEZAVISNA PROCJENA: Ovo nije samo bezbjednosni skandal -- riječ je o sistemskom neuspjehu upravljanja koji razotkriva šupljinu reformske priče Crne Gore u najkritičnijem trenutku njenog procesa pristupanja EU.

ČINJENICE SU PORAŽAVAJUĆE. Miloš Medenica, vođa kriminalne organizacije od 16 članova umješane u krijumčarenje droge i cigareta, posljednji put je potvrđeno boravio na adresi kućnog pritvora u 17:30 sati, 27. januara 2026. godine. Do trenutka izricanja presude od 10 godina i 2 mjeseca sledećeg dana, već je bio nestao. Viši sud Podgorica potvrdio je da su policija bila zakonski obavezna da spriječi bjekstvo. U roku od otprilike jedne sedmice, bivša specijalna tužiteljka Lidija Mitrović takođe se nije javila na izdržavanje sedmomjesečne kazne. Dva bjegstva visokoprofilnih lica u brzom nizu predstavljaju obrazac, a ne slučajnost.

PROPUST NA VIŠEM NIVOU O KOME NIKO NE GOVORI. Ministarstva jednoglasno osuđuju bjekstvo, ali nijedno ne postavlja očigledna prethodna pitanja: zašto je Medenica uopšte bio u kućnom pritvoru? Pušten je iz pritvora u Spužu u novembru 2025. (dokumentovano od strane HRA), uprkos tome što je vodio kriminalnu organizaciju, a uprkos tome što njegova majka -- bivša predsjednica Vrhovnog suda Vesna Medenica, sankcionisana od strane američkog State Departmenta u septembru 2025. zbog korupcije -- provela je dvije decenije gradeći mreže sudskog pokroviteljstva. Rizik od bijega bio je savršeno jasan. Pravosuđe koje je dopustilo kućni pritvor za ovog optuženog snosi odgovornost zajedno sa policijom koja ga nije spriječila u bjekstvu.

VLADIN NEODGOVOR JE GORI OD SAMOG BJEKSTVA. Predsjednik Milatović je bio u pravu kada je zahtijevao razrješenja. Odbijanje direktora policije da podnese ostavku, nakon što je sam Viši sud potvrdio da je policija snosila obavezu izvršenja, čin je institucionalnog prkosa. Odlazak premijera Spajića u UAE nakon jedne jedine izjave nije samo loša slika -- to je signal da šef vlade ne može ili neće vršiti autoritet nad sopstvenim bezbjednosnim aparatom. Karakterizacija bivšeg premijera Abazovića o "totalnom kolapsu sistema bezbjednosti" potkrijepljena je činjenicama.

DIMENZIJA SLOBODE ŠTAMPE PRODUBLJUJE KRIZU. Pozivanje kolumniste Duška Kovačevića od strane policije na razgovor, 16. februara, zbog njegovog teksta "Bolje od bijega" u kome je kritikovao direktora policije, udžbenički je primjer autoritarnog skretanja pažnje. HRA je dokumentovala da mu nije dostavljen pisani poziv sa obrazloženjem, da Kovačević nije obavješten o pravu na advokata, te da nije naveden nijedan konkretan krivičnopravni osnov. Osnovno državno tužilaštvo je naknadno potvrdilo da nisu utvrđeni elementi krivičnog djela. Radi se o bezbjednosnom aparatu koji koristi svoju izvršnu moć protiv kritičara, a istovremeno propušta da je primijeni na bjegunce -- jezivo izvrtanje institucionalne svrhe.

UTICAJ NA PRISTUPANJE EU. Crna Gora je dobila pozitivan IBAR za poglavlja 23 i 24 u junu 2024. i zatvorila poglavlje 32 u januaru 2026, gradeći istinski zamah. Taj zamah je sada ozbiljno ugrožen. Komesarka EU Koš upozorila je da "najtežo tek predstoji" kada su u pitanju poglavlja o vladavini prava. Bjekstvo Medenice pruža Evropskoj komisiji upravo onu vrstu konkretnog propusta u sprovođenju zakona koji opravdava skepticizam u pogledu institucionalnih kapaciteta Crne Gore. Izvještaj o napretku EK za 2025. već je upozorio da "bez značajnog ubrzanja reformi, zatvaranje svih poglavlja do kraja 2026. ostaće nedostižno". Ovaj skandal pomjera kazaljku u pogrešnom smjeru. Sankcije SAD-a Vesni Medenici u septembru 2025. zbog omogućavanja trgovine drogom putem sudske korupcije, u kombinaciji sa bjekstvom njenog sina, stvaraju kumulativni problem međunarodnog kredibiliteta.

PROCJENA MINISTARSKIH EVALUACIJA. Sedam ministarskih evaluacija suštinski je temeljito i dobro potkrijepljeno. Pravilno identifikuju ključni rizik po pristupanje EU, citiraju relevantne međunarodne izvore (OCCRP, Balkan Insight, HRA, američki State Department) i dovode skandal u vezu sa odgovarajućim oblastima. Međutim, njihova analitička vrijednost umanjena je trima problemima. Prvo, jednoobraznost ocjena (sve između 2 i 3) u ministarstvima sa veoma različitim nadležnostima ukazuje na koordinaciju, a ne na nezavisnu analizu. Ministarstvo pravde i Ministarstvo unutrašnjih poslova imaju direktnu mandatnu relevantnost i njihove ocjene od 2 su dobro kalibrisane; ali ocjena 3 Ministarstva turizma i Ministarstva prosvjete za bezbjednosni skandal djeluje kao mehaničko ispunjavanje obrazaca. Drugo, svako ministarstvo pribijega istom analitičkom okviru centriranom na pristupanje EU, koji, iako validan, daje repetitivnu analizu umjesto stvarnog uvida specifičnog za datu oblast. Treće, i ključno, nijedno ministarstvo ne predlaže konkretne reformske mjere usmjerene ka budućnosti -- dijagnostikuju bolest, ali ne propisuju lijek. Nijedan dokument ne pominje elektronski nadzor, GPS narukvice, obavezni pritvor prije izricanja presude za optužene s visokim rizikom bjekstva, niti bilo koje druge specifične institucionalne mjere. Radi se o analizi bez preporuka koje se mogu primijeniti, što ograničava njenu vrijednost u upravljanju.

DUBLJE PITANJE. Slučaj Medenica otkriva da li su crnogorski procesi za korupciju suštinske reforme ili performativne vježbe. Procesuiranje i osuđivanje sina bivše predsjednice Vrhovnog suda bio je istorijski uspjeh. Međutim, presuda koja se ne može izvršiti gora je od nepostojanja presude -- ona dokazuje državni kapacitet za istragu i procesuiranje, istovremeno demonstrirajući nesposobnost da se kazne izvrše. Međunarodna zajednica i crnogorski građani ostaju sa najgorim mogućim signalom: sistem može identifikovati kriminalce na vlasti, ali ih zapravo ne može pozvati na odgovornost. Ovaj jaz između procesuiranja i izvršenja centralni je propust upravljanja koji ministarstva identifikuju, ali ga ne objašnjavaju na adekvatan način.

OCJENA: Vladino postupanje u slučaju bjekstva Medenice -- obilježeno policijskim neuspjehom, šutnjom zvaničnika, odsutnošću premijera, odbijanjem preuzimanja odgovornosti i zastrašivanjem novinara -- zaslužuje ocjenu 2 od 10. Jedini razlog zašto nije dobilo ocjenu 1 jeste to što su originalno procesuiranje i osuda mreže Medenica predstavljali istinski institucionalni napor, čak i ako je taj napor naknadno poništen propustom u izvršenju.
blind_spots_mne: Nijedno ministarstvo nije dovelo u pitanje zašto je Medenica uopšte bio u kućnom pritvoru umjesto u zatvoru. Pušten je iz pritvora u Spužu u novembru 2025, uprkos tome što mu je prijetila kazna od 10 godina zatvora zbog vođenja kriminalne organizacije. Odluka pravosuđa da odobri kućni pritvor optuženom organizovanog kriminala s visokim rizikom od bjekstva -- čija je majka bila bivša predsjednica Vrhovnog suda s dokumentovanim vezama sa njegovom kriminalnom mrežom -- predstavlja uočljiv propust na višem nivou koji je svako ministarstvo previdjelo.

Mogućnost namjernog pomaganja u bjekstvu od strane insajdera pomenuta je samo uzgredno od strane Ministarstva unutrašnjih poslova. S obzirom na to da je Medenicina kriminalna mreža uključivala policijskog službenika (Darka Lalovića) koji je, prema Sky ECC transkriptima, pomagao u organizovanju kupovine kokaina, te s obzirom na to da je njegova majka vodila Vrhovni sud gotovo dvije decenije gradeći mreže pokroviteljstva, pitanje da li su policijski ili sudski insajderi aktivno pomogli bjekstvo zaslužilo je centralnu, a ne periferni analizu.

Nijedno ministarstvo nije ispitalo dimenziju koalicione politike. Premijer Spajić vodi krhku koalicionu vladu koja je već doživjela krize oko imenovanja direktora policije (Balkan Insight je izvijestio o raskolu unutar vlade po pitanju imenovanja šefa policije u martu 2024). Nemogućnost razrješenja direktora policije možda odražava koaliciona ograničenja, a ne puku ravnodušnost -- razlika koja ima praktične implikacije za izglede reformi.

Nijedno ministarstvo nije predložilo konkretne institucionalne reforme radi sprečavanja ponavljanja. Elektronske narukvice za praćenje, GPS praćenje, obavezni pritvor prije izricanja presude za optužene s rizikom od bjekstva i reforma protokola za sprovođenje kućnog pritvora standardne su mjere u državama članicama EU. Odsustvo bilo kakvih preporuka usmjerenih ka budućnosti čini evaluacije dijagnostičkim, ali ne i preskriptivnim.

Konkretan odredišni premijerov cilj -- UAE -- primijećen je, ali nikada nije doveden u pitanje. Crna Gora ima složene finansijske odnose sa zalivskim državama, a tajming posjete UAE usred domaće krize ovakvog razmjera postavlja pitanja o suprotstavljenim prioritetima i zavisnostima u spoljnoj politici kojima se nijedno ministarstvo nije bavilo.

Nijedno ministarstvo nije analiziralo efekat zastrašivanja na tekuće predmete organizovanog kriminala. Ako drugi visokoprofilni optuženi vide da osuđeni kriminalni boss može nestati nekažnjeno, racionalni kalkulus za bjekstvo umjesto izdržavanja kazne dramatično se mijenja. Ova dimenzija postavljanja presedana -- koja potencijalno utiče na desetine aktivnih predmeta -- u potpunosti je previđena.

Regionalna dimenzija bila je nedovoljno istražena. Medenica je vjerovatno prešao međunarodnu granicu, ali nijedno ministarstvo nije analiziralo u koju je zemlju vjerovatno otišao, šta to otkriva o međugraničnim prazninama u primjeni zakona, niti da li susjedne države mogu biti saučesnici. Interpolova potjernica podrazumijeva bjekstvo u inostranstvo, a regionalni propusti u saradnji ostaju neistraženi.

Svih sedam ministarstava ocijenilo je odluku između 2 i 3 od 10, s identičnim ocjenama 'negativno' ili 'izrazito negativno' i izuzetno sličnim analitičkim okvirima centriranim na pristupanje EU. Ova jednoobraznost u ministarstvima s veoma različitim nadležnostima (Prosvjeta, Zdravlje, Životna sredina, Turizam) ukazuje na koordinisano slanje poruka, a ne na istinski nezavisnu analizu specifičnu za svaku oblast. Zaista nezavisno Ministarstvo prosvjete možda bi se više fokusiralo na uticaj na građansko obrazovanje; Ministarstvo zdravlja na konkretne slučajeve korupcije u zdravstvenom sistemu.
eu_chapter_relevance_mne: Pog. 23 Pravosuđe i temeljna prava -- Bjekstvo osuđenog vođe organizovanog kriminala čija je majka bila bivša predsjednica Vrhovnog suda, u kombinaciji sa prethodnom sudskom odlukom o odobravanju kućnog pritvora optuženom sa visokim rizikom od bjekstva, direktno podriva završna mjerila koja zahtijevaju uvjerljiv bilans u procesuiranju i izvršenju kazni za organizovani kriminal i korupciju na visokom nivou. Napredak IBAR-a iz juna 2024. u opasnosti je da bude poništen.

Pog. 24 Pravda, sloboda i bezbjednost -- Propust policije da sprovede kućni pritvor, neuspjeh granične kontrole koji je omogućio međunarodno bjekstvo (o čemu svjedoči Interpolova potjernica), odbijanje direktora policije da preuzme odgovornost i gotovo istovremeno bjekstvo bivše specijalne tužiteljke Mitrović otkrivaju sistemske slabosti unutrašnje bezbjednosti, kapaciteta za sprovođenje zakona i prekogranične saradnje koje su ključne za završna mjerila zatvaranja ovog poglavlja.

Pog. 10 Informaciono društvo i mediji -- Pozivanje kolumniste Duška Kovačevića od strane policije zbog kritike direktora policije, bez odgovarajućeg pravnog osnova ili proceduralnih zaštita kako je dokumentovala HRA, predstavlja zastrašivanje slobode štampe koje je u suprotnosti sa standardima EKLJP i zahtjevima slobode medija koji se procjenjuju u okviru ovog privremeno zatvorenog poglavlja. To bi moglo navesti EU da preispita status poglavlja.

Pog. 31 Vanjska, bezbjednosna i odbrambena politika -- Aktivna Interpolova potjernica za Medenicu i regionalna dimenzija njegovog bjekstva postavljaju pitanja o kapacitetu Crne Gore za međunarodnu policijsku saradnju i njenu pouzdanost kao partnera u EU bezbjednosnim okvirima.

counter_proposal.title: Emergency Rule of Law and Institutional Accountability Recovery Plan
counter_proposal.executive_summary: The AI Government would replace the current policy of silence and evasion with an immediate three-track response: command accountability within 72 hours (dismissal of the Police Director, criminal investigation into enforcement failures), systemic reform within 30 days (mandatory GPS electronic monitoring for high-risk detainees, border security audit, dedicated sentence enforcement unit), and proactive EU damage control (remedial action plan presented to the European Commission within 30 days to preserve the Chapter 23/24 closing timeline). This crisis must be treated as both a rule-of-law emergency and an economic threat to Montenegro's EUR 3.79 billion fiscal framework, EU accession trajectory, and investor confidence.
counter_proposal.detailed_proposal:
This unified counter-proposal takes the Ministry of Interior and Ministry of Justice proposals as its backbone, since the Medenica escape is fundamentally a law enforcement and rule-of-law failure. It integrates the economic damage-containment framework from the Ministry of Finance, the EU accession crisis management approach from the Ministry of EU Integration, and targeted institutional resilience measures from the sectoral ministries (Health, Education, Environment, Tourism) to create a comprehensive response.

TRACK ONE: IMMEDIATE ACCOUNTABILITY (0-72 HOURS)

The Police Director must be dismissed or suspended within 72 hours of initiating this plan. The High Court of Podgorica has explicitly confirmed that the Police Administration was legally obligated to prevent Medenica's escape, and the Director's public claim that police had no further obligation beyond noting a breach is legally untenable. Accountability at the command level is not optional -- it is a prerequisite for every other element of this plan.
